🔍 *ENRICH REVIEW*  \[🔧 STILL IN PROGRESS\]

*Name:* Brampton Bryan Castle
*Country:* england
*Region:* the midlands
*County:* Herefordshire
*Type:* castle
*Period:* 14th century
*Coords:* 52.3478, -2.9263
*Wikipedia:* https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brampton_Bryan_Castle
*Maps:* https://www.google.com/maps/search/Brampton%20Bryan%20Castle/@52.3417,-2.89,15z
*Main Image:* https://img.castlecore.uk/brampton-bryan-castle.jpg
*Gallery:* 4 images
*Image Source:* CDN
*Has Wikipedia:* yes
*Has Google Places:* unknown
*Enrich Notes:* needs more research 

*Description:*
Brampton Bryan Castle is a ruined medieval castle in the village of Brampton Bryan in north-western Herefordshire, England, 50m south of the River Teme. The castle guarded an important route from Ludlow along the Teme Valley to Knighton and on into Central Wales.

_Listing 1 of 252_
